input.headline: George Washington faces Georgetown on 3-game losing streak

raw.headline: George Washington faces Georgetown on 3-game losing streak

winner: George Washington just lost three in a row—turns out he didn’t know his way around a court either.

candidates.text: Why did George Washington’s team have a losing streak? Because they kept getting *Hamilton*-ian.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'Hamilton' and the historical context are clever and specific, with a good surprise factor."}

candidates.text: George Washington's losing streak is so long, he hasn't won a battle since crossing the Delaware... and that was just his team's halftime score.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a clever historical reference with a modern twist, creating surprise and humor."}

candidates.text: George Washington lost three in a row—turns out his famous 'can't tell a lie' policy was more about basketball than cherry trees.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on historical figures with a sports angle, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Georgetown's three-game winning streak just got *Washington*-ed.
